description: Cloud-based CRM platform for managing customer relationships across sales, service, marketing, commerce, and related business workflows.

authentication.notes: Salesforce supports multiple authentication methods including OAuth 2.0 flows (Web Server, JWT Bearer, Client Credentials) and traditional username/password with security tokens or MFA.

mcp.transport_or_hosting_notes: The provided documentation does not mention MCP or its integration with Salesforce.

evidence.page_title: Salesforce: The #1 Agentic AI CRM | Salesforce AU
evidence.excerpt: Salesforce is the#1 AI CRM(customer relationship management) platform.

evidence.claim: Salesforce is a unified CRM platform powered by AI and data for various departments.
evidence.page_title: Salesforce: The #1 Agentic AI CRM | Salesforce AU
evidence.excerpt: Salesforce is the#1 AI CRM(customer relationship management) platform. We bring companies and customers together by providing a unified set of applications, powered by agentic AI and data. This enables every department to work as one, including sales, service, marketing, commerce, and IT.

evidence.claim: Salesforce uses OAuth 2.0 for API authorization.
evidence.page_title: Authorization Through External Client Apps or Connected Apps and OAuth 2.0 | REST API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: To implement this authorization, use either an external client app or a connected app and an OAuth 2.0 authorization flow.

evidence.claim: Salesforce supports username and password authentication with a security token.
evidence.page_title: Security and the API | SOAP API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: If the permission isn’t set, users must add their security token to the end of their password to log in.

evidence.page_title: Security and the API | SOAP API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: If the user has the Multi-Factor Authentication for User Interface Logins permission, the Salesforce login process prompts the user for an identity verification method in addition to their username and password.

evidence.claim: Salesforce supports OAuth Client Credentials flow.
evidence.page_title: Using the Client Credentials Flow for Easier API Authentication | Salesforce Developers Blog
evidence.excerpt: Salesforce APIs now support OAuth Client Credentials

evidence.claim: Client applications must use external client apps or connected apps with OAuth 2.0 to access REST API resources.
evidence.page_title: Authorization Through External Client Apps or Connected Apps and OAuth 2.0 | REST API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: For a client application to access REST API resources, it must be authorized as a safe visitor. To implement this authorization, use either an external client app or a connected app and an OAuth 2.0 authorization flow.

evidence.claim: External client apps enable third-party applications to integrate with Salesforce.
evidence.page_title: Authorization | Salesforce DX Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: Salesforce CLI requires an external client app in the org that you're authorizing. An external client app is a packageable framework that enables a third-party application (Salesforce CLI) to integrate with Salesforce by using APIs and security protocols.

evidence.claim: API access is restricted by the permissions of the logged-in user.
evidence.page_title: Security and the API | SOAP API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: Client apps can query or update only those objects and fields to which they have access via the permissions of the logged-in user.

evidence.claim: The Salesforce Platform provides a rich API portfolio for integration.
evidence.page_title: The Salesforce Platform - Transformed for Tomorrow | Get Started with Fundamentals | Fundamentals | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: The Salesforce Platform prioritizes a rich and coherent API portfolio that covers everything that can be done via Salesforce-native user interfaces. This allows developers and partners to leverage and re-compose the platform’s functionality for integrating systems or building new user experiences.

evidence.claim: The platform is built to host multiple customers using a multitenant architecture.
evidence.page_title: The Salesforce Platform - Transformed for Tomorrow | Get Started with Fundamentals | Fundamentals | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: Multitenant: All services and infrastructure are built to host multiple customers. This provides a strategic pattern for scaling with use, as well as standardizing on a common high-bar of availability and security regardless of the size of our customers.

evidence.page_title: Developer Documentation | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: Apex is based on the syntax of Java and optimized for Salesforce features like multi-tenancy, security, backward compatibility, and scalability.

evidence.page_title: Introduction to REST API | REST API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: REST API provides you with programmatic access to your data in Salesforce.

evidence.page_title: GraphQL API | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: GraphQL API provides a single endpoint that returns exactly what you request for.

evidence.page_title: Introduction to REST API | REST API Developer Guide | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: REST API provides you with programmatic access to your data in Salesforce.

evidence.page_title: What is GraphQL API? | Get Started | GraphQL API | Salesforce Developers
evidence.excerpt: Salesforce GraphQL API is available in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions.

evidence.excerpt: The flexibility and scalability of REST API make it an excellent choice for integrating Salesforce into your applications and for performing complex operations on a large scale.
